headline: Beans at peak — harvest this morning before storms hit.
weather_notes: Run both zones this morning — 2 days dry at 99°F is past the 1-day heat threshold; hold the next cycle if Wednesday's 60% storms actually deliver.
Pick beans and blackberries this morning — tonight's 50% and Wednesday's 60% storm chances risk knocking pods and bruising ripe fruit on the cane.
Apply fish emulsion to e1–e4 before noon — Wednesday's 60% storm window will help wash it into root zones.
Finish all outdoor tasks before noon — 99°F heat and afternoon thunderstorms close the outdoor work window through Wednesday.

surfaced_tasks.title: Tip new blackberry primocanes if 3–4 ft tall
surfaced_tasks.reason: 11 days overdue; canes are 37+ days of growth since the Jun 14 observation — this morning before afternoon storms is the week's clearest outdoor window.

suggested_tasks.title: Harvest c2–c4 beans — pods at maturity
suggested_tasks.description: Jade Bush (c3/c4) and Red Swan (c2) are 54 days in, right at the 55-day window; pick today before tonight's 50% storms knock mature pods.

suggested_tasks.title: Feed e1–e4 tomatoes with fish emulsion
suggested_tasks.description: 31-day transplants have no logged fertilizer; apply this morning so Wednesday's 60% storm chance helps wash it into root zones.
